author: Mark Frost
text: 2026-07-18 2-node isolation result: gap is GATEWAY-SIDE. Reflashed the gateway to pin ONLY the T1000-E. Result over 90s: T1000-E coap.ok still 0, and critically l2rx.verified/injected FROZE (106->106) while rejected kept climbing (T-Echo frames). So since the isolation reflash the T1000-E verified ZERO gateway frames. Because the T1000-E demonstrably CAN hear+verify gateway transmissions (verified climbed in the 2-peer test when it overheard gateway->T-Echo responses), a frozen counter means the gateway is transmitting nothing to it -> the gateway is NOT responding to the T1000-E's GETs. Gateway console (isolation) confirms it pins 8a1a..2cab + CoAP server AUTOSTART, 6 Schnorr auth-fails in 45s (T-Echo frames, now unpinned; possibly some T1000-E frames too). So the T1000-E RX path is fully exonerated (radio+L2+inject all proven); the failure is the gateway's handling of the T1000-E's GET: either it is not accepting the GET (Schnorr/replay) so it never reaches the CoAP server, or it accepts but the CoAP response never TXes back (routing/ND to the T1000-E static neighbor, or half-duplex TX drop). NEXT: gateway-side instrumentation - add the l2rx STATS group read-out on the gateway (it already compiles there) + a 'coap responses sent' counter, but the gateway (ESP32/CP2102) has no stable SMP channel (console is reset-destructive), so either wire SMP-over-CDC on the gateway or bump its lichen_l2/coap log to DBG to watch RX-ok + response TX for the T1000-E. Suspect the T1000-E's static-neighbor / GET source-address handling at the gateway differs from the T-Echo's.
